notes: Spec asserts: ready → working, state stays working across all retry cycles (no flicker to ready or waiting between attempts), final working → ready. Vibe is a single-birth adapter (session id = the session_<ts>_<hash> dir via SessionIDFromPath); session_birth is anchored to 'start' UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession or the real session (whichever the process scanner mints first) satisfies the birth, and the turn phases anchor forward so the matcher lands the working transitions on the real session. The recipe drives a deterministic failing-then-passing bash sequence (bash 'false' then bash 'echo recovered') in ONE turn: the parser sees one user_message, multiple assistant(tool_calls)→tool_result cycles, then a text-only assistant (turn_done). A failing tool_result carries no IsError for vibe (the adapter reads no status field) so it is just continued activity — turn_done is the only ready settle. The intermediate_working phase pins the no-flicker invariant with duration_at_least_ms 2000 — long enough to span both retry cycles given typical vibe latency. If the parser ever surfaced a tool_result as a transition to ready or waiting, this phase fires. observations: agent=mistral-vibe; model=mistral-medium-3.5 (confirmed live meta.json config.active_model default, passes NormalizeModelName unchanged); tokens_nonzero=true (turn_done emits stats.context_tokens); cost_nonzero=false (the adapter never reads session_cost).

text: Session appears in ready at launch (pre-session proc-<PID> or the session_<ts> id, whichever arrives first)
User prompt drives ready → working (spec bullet 1)

State stays working across the failing bash 'false' and the corrective bash 'echo recovered' — the failing tool_result (no IsError for vibe) must NOT flicker the session to ready or waiting before the recovery cycle (spec bullet 2)
After the final successful attempt, the text-only assistant reply settles turn_done: working → ready (spec bullet 3); final state ready
